nameMeanings.name: Brudenell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: ethnic name from Old Norman French Bretonnel, a diminutive of Bret ‘Breton’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell was christened in 1322, in Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son. He died in his hometown, and was buried in Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell a été baptisé en 1322, à Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell wurde 1322 in Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom getauft. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell è stato battezzato nel 1322 nel Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenellは1322年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアエインhオで洗礼を受けた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアエインhオにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell(은)는 1322년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 아이호에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 아이호에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell foi batizado em 1322, em Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell был крещен в 1322 г. в местности аино, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности аино, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Brudenell se bautizó en 1322, en Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Aynhoe, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
